SEQUENCE.COMMENT: mimedocToEmaildoc - convert a mimedoc to a simplified email document
from mimeData, start an emailDoc with the basic information from the email

MAP.COMMENT: find the different spellings of Message-ID

INVOKE.COMMENT: -0660 with -1100 the 0660 represents number of minutes offset rather than a time offset
-05:00 with -0500 colons not permitted in timezone offset

BRANCH.COMMENT: set a date conversion pattern based on what we see in the format of the Date

MAP.NAME: /^[A-Za-z]+\, [0-9][0-9] [A-Za-z]+ [0-9][0-9] [0-9]+\:[0-9]+\:[0-9]+ /
MAP.COMMENT: EEE, dd MMM yy HH:mm:ss Z Mon, 26 Aug 24 19:03:07 -0500

Values.value: EEE, dd MMM yy HH:mm:ss Z

MAP.NAME: /^[A-Za-z]+\,.*[0-9]+\:[0-9]+\:[0-9]+\s/
MAP.COMMENT: EEE, dd MMM yyyy HH:mm:ss Z Mon, 26 Aug 2024 19:03:07 -0500

Values.value: EEE, dd MMM yyyy HH:mm:ss Z

MAP.NAME: /^[A-Za-z]+\,.*[0-9]+\:[0-9]+\s/
MAP.COMMENT: EEE, dd MMM yyyy HH:mm Z Mon, 26 Aug 2024 19:03 -0500

Values.value: EEE, dd MMM yyyy HH:mm Z

MAP.NAME: /^[0-9]+\s[A-Za-z]+\s[0-9]+\s[0-9]+\:[0-9]+\:[0-9]+\sUT$/
MAP.COMMENT: dd MMM yyyy HH:mm:ss Z 27 Aug 2024 10:59:38 UT

MAP.NAME: /^[0-9]+\s[A-Za-z]+\s[0-9]+\s[0-9]+\:[0-9]+\sUT$/
MAP.COMMENT: dd MMM yy HH:mm Z 27 Aug 24 10:59 UT

MAP.NAME: /^[0-9]+\s[A-Za-z]+/
MAP.COMMENT: dd MMM yyyy HH:mm:ss Z 27 Aug 2024 10:59:35 -0400
extract the important fields from the mimeData ==> emailDoc
if the Thread-Topic (Subject) is blank then use the actual Subject field from the mimeDoc
remove any CRs and LFs from the From, To, Cc - replace any CRs / LFs / spaces in Subject with a single space - replace century of 00 with 20
replace the center portion with comma <abc@xyz.com>, <def@tuv.com> ==> <abc@xyz.com,def@tuv.com>
remove the leading and trailing portions <abc@xyz.com,def@tuv.com> ==> abc@xyz.com,def@tuv.com
finally lowercase the From, the To and the Cc, leaving just simple, easy to parse addresses for all of them

INVOKE.COMMENT: decode a subject line that is base64 encoded ( starts with =?utf-8?B? and ends with ?= )

MAP.COMMENT: start by setting the Subject to items[0], which for normal Subjects, is the regular Subject line

LOOP.COMMENT: then loop over any of multiple embedded encoding references in the Subject

BRANCH.COMMENT: skip the first entry, which will always be blank

INVOKE.COMMENT: then concatenate the decoded result to the Subject

MAP.COMMENT: --- the basic email info is in place, now get the Body and all attachments ---

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: from mimeData, process all attachments, including the first 'attachment' which is the email body itself ==> emailDoc/Body, emailDoc/Attachments

INVOKE.COMMENT: find out how many parts are in the MIME object

RETRY.COMMENT: loop over the mime parts, the first of which is the body of the main email, followed by all of the attachments

SEQUENCE.NAME: loop == 0
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: process the standard body of the message ==> emailDoc/Body

BRANCH.COMMENT: if there is content at this point, set it as the body of the email doc

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: this section is supposed to try and get the Filename, but sometimes the filename is under the Content-Type in the next section below

BRANCH.COMMENT: choose one of the above two (or both) as a starter filename of the attachment

INVOKE.COMMENT: ; filename = with ; filename= remove a space before the =
p1 += 9 to get past the filename=

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: this section is supposed to only be for the Filetype, but sometimes, the filename is here instead

MAP.COMMENT: mimeHeader/Content-Type => Filetype with a default of application/unknown also get Content-Transfer-Encoding

INVOKE.COMMENT: p1 += 5 to get past the name=
p1 += 5 to get past the name=

BRANCH.COMMENT: if we find our special string from fixBadMimeData that indicates this was base64 encoded...

SEQUENCE.COMMENT: regardless of where we found the Filename above, we still have to clean it up

MAP.COMMENT: attachment; filename*0="Excel Generate Attachment - Template 2_202409201105452193228"; filename*1=.xlsx

BRANCH.COMMENT: we are piecing together the filename from the odd looking filename*0=abc filename*1=xyz construct shown above

INVOKE.COMMENT: remove ;anything from Filename usually this is a size=nnn; or something else meaningless
.PDF with .pdf but only at the end of the string
.PDF" with .pdf" but only at the end of the string
now get the attachment itself as a Bytestream
extraction complete, returning: From: %From% To: %To% Subject: %Subject% Date: %Date% (%numAtts% attachments)

Values.value: extraction complete, returning: From: %emailDoc/From% To: %emailDoc/To% Subject: %emailDoc/Subject% Date: %emailDoc/Date% (%numAtts% attachments)
